order.name: Intermediary Ingredients First
order.detail: Whenever possible, work from upgraded ingredients instead of raw materials. Intermediary items usually give you better synthesis results and more flexibility in the long run.

order.name: Attack Consumables Early
order.detail: Practical consumables are often the fastest way to get immediate value from synthesis. They can keep battles manageable while your gear is still developing.

order.name: Wait for Recipe Level 3 Before Crafting Gear
order.detail: Recipe levels improve synthesis options, so it is usually worth investing particles before you lock in final gear pieces.

order.name: Level Recipes to 5 and 8 for More Slots
order.detail: Recipe progression stays relevant throughout the game. Revisit older recipes when you have the resources to push them further.

coreGuide.howto: Use your strongest ingredients here when you want the finished item itself to be better. Quality is one of the most important synthesis values to watch.
coreGuide.priority: High early. Raise your quality ceiling first whenever possible.

coreGuide.howto: Matching ingredient types and strong resonance help improve effect performance. This is especially important for items you use often.
coreGuide.priority: High for combat items. Effects scale directly into usefulness.

coreGuide.howto: Traits become more important once you have stronger materials and more room to optimize. Treat this as a later layer of crafting refinement.
coreGuide.priority: Mid-to-late. Prioritize only after your core items are stable.

tips.text: Ingredient placement matters. Core effects and slot effects are not interchangeable, so read each effect carefully before deciding where to place a material.
High-value intermediary ingredients are often worth saving for your most important crafts instead of spending them immediately.
Once your crafting loop is established, duplication and production facilities can save a huge amount of time.
A backup save is a smart idea before expensive late-game synthesis, especially if you want to experiment with different builds.
